text:
Once upon a timeless night, a wise old owl named Aethelwin settled into his usual perch by the oak tree. He had watched the world change over eons but remained unchanged, an expert on the whispers of time.

A ethereal moonbeam found its way down from above and danced along the silvery bark of Aethelwin's tree. There, in the soft glow, a tiny, dewy-eyed creature peered at its reflection in the owl's eye.

"Hello," it chirruped. The tiny thing was a forest mouse, seeking knowledge beyond its years.

"A wise old owl once told me that there's no time for children," Aethelwin uttered. "But I must share what I know."

The mouse hopped closer. "Share?"

Aethelwin chuckled softly. "Sure. 'Once upon a time there was just me, and time was a smooth stream. Then one day...'" he paused. "Some rabbits created clocks."

"
